deployment_question: How does the model perform on chemical clusters separated by fingerprint similarity?
definition: Cluster molecules with a fingerprint similarity rule, such as Butina/Taylor-Butina or chemical similarity clustering, then hold out clusters to reduce train-test structural overlap.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: samples a lower-overlap chemical similarity operating point than random or scaffold splits

implementation_recipe: Compute molecular fingerprints with recorded settings.
Cluster molecules using a similarity threshold or precomputed similarity graph.
Assign whole clusters to train or test and report residual nearest-train similarity.
quality_gates: Report fingerprint type, radius, bit length, and clustering threshold.
Compare the split against scaffold and continuous SPECTRA curves.
failure_modes: A single threshold can produce unstable or giant clusters.
Fingerprint choice changes the operating point.
Activity cliffs may remain within low-overlap clusters.

citations.title: UMAP-based clustering split for rigorous evaluation of AI models for virtual screening
citations.evidence: Compares Butina clustering against scaffold and UMAP splits for virtual-screening evaluation.

citations.title: Evaluating Machine Learning Models for Molecular Property Prediction on OOD Data
citations.evidence: Finds chemical similarity clustering is a challenging OOD generation strategy.
